name: Raymer Sizing Loop
summary: A conceptual aircraft sizing workflow built around weight fractions, wing loading, power or thrust loading, and constraint analysis.
whenToUseIt: Use it during early aircraft design when you need a first credible answer for gross weight, wing area, and installed propulsion before detailed geometry exists.
steps: Estimate mission fuel fraction from the mission profile.
Estimate empty weight fraction from historical data and configuration class.
Solve for takeoff gross weight using payload, crew, fuel, and empty weight relationships.
Use constraint charts to choose wing loading and thrust or power loading.
Translate the sizing result into wing area, engine size, and basic geometry.
example: For a 19-seat regional aircraft, a designer can use mission range, reserve fuel, and field length requirements to narrow the concept to a feasible takeoff mass and wing area before building any detailed CAD.
